zh.lifeSummary: 當Johann Georg Lang於1691年3月29日出生在德國賀瑟奧登瓦爾德可萊斯貝爾費爾登時，他的父親Peter Lang33歲，母親M. Catharina Kumpf29歲。他於1715年1月16日與Eva Steck在多易傑斯 萊奇格洛斯黑爾左圖姆 賀瑟可萊斯 艾爾巴賀貝爾費爾登結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1767年11月10日在多易傑斯 萊奇格洛斯黑爾左圖姆 賀瑟可萊斯 艾爾巴賀貝爾費爾登享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Johann Georg Lang于1691年3月29日出生在德国賀瑟奧登瓦爾德可萊斯貝爾費爾登时，他的父亲Peter Lang33岁，母亲M. Catharina Kumpf29岁。他于1715年1月16日与Eva Steck在多易傑斯 萊奇格洛斯黑爾左圖姆 賀瑟可萊斯 艾爾巴賀貝爾費爾登结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1767年11月10日在多易傑斯 萊奇格洛斯黑爾左圖姆 賀瑟可萊斯 艾爾巴賀貝爾費爾登享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lang
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, English, German, Dutch, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic); Swedish (also Lång): nickname for a tall person, from Older Scots, Middle English, Middle High German, Middle Dutch, and Danish lang ‘long’, Swedish lång. The surname of (Middle High) German origin is also found in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, Hungary, Poland, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia.

Hungarian (Láng): from láng ‘flame’, hence probably a nickname for a passionate person, or a man with a fighting spirit. Alternatively, it may be an indirect occupational name for a smith or someone who worked with fire. This surname is also found in Slovenia, where it is spelled Lang, and in Slovakia, where it is spelled in both ways.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 郎, an ancient term to refer to one's lover or son: (i) from the placename Lang (郎), the name of a city, the living place of Fei Bo, the grandson of the Duke Yi of Lu (ruler of the state of Lu, reigned 815–807  BC ). Some of his descendants later changed their original surname Fei to Lang (郎). (ii) borne by some families from the state of the South Huns (48–216  AD , located in northern China).

variants.name: Laing
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lang  .

variants.name: Langer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Horst, Kurt, Otto, Dieter, Franz, Gerhard, Manfred, Fritz, Siegfried, Alois, Angelika.

German, Dutch, Danish, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a tall man, from an inflected form of Lang . In the Franconian dialect of German this was also a term for an unskilled laborer (shortened from Handlanger). Compare Longer and Longie .
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Landgari, composed of the elements lant ‘land, territory’ + gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’.

variants.name: Leng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Chu, Chan, Chea, Chi-Cheng, Gang, Heang, Kao, Leung, Oi, Ping, Shek.  Southeast Asian Sophal, Chhay, Chhun, Da, Hoa, Khuon, Linh, Long, Thao, Loeuth, Oeun, Pheap, Sokheng, Sovann.
English: nickname, either from Middle English leng(e) ‘ling’, for someone who dealt in or was thought to resemble this fish. Compare Pike , Tench , Dace , and other fish names.
English: variant of   Laing  .

variants.name: Lange
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Erwin, Heinz, Horst, Otto, Wolfgang, Ernst, Helmut, Hermann, Manfred, Reinhold.
German, Scandinavian, and Dutch: variant of   Lang   ‘long’. This surname is also found in Poland and in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   De Lange  .
French: variant, with fused definite article l', of Ange 2. Compare Longe 1.

variants.name: Langhans
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Markus, Helmut, Wilhelm.
German: distinguishing nickname for a tall man (see   Lang  ) called Hans.

variants.name: Lenger
meanings: German: nickname for a tall man; a variant of   Lang  .

variants.name: Langen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bodo, Gunther, Heinz, Rainer.
German: patronymic from   Lang  .
German: habitational name from any of several places so named.

variants.name: Langel
meanings: Swiss German and East German: from the nickname Langel ‘the tall one’, a diminutive of Lang .
German: habitational name from any of several places called Langel.

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vernacular form of   Johannes .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: Georg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When Johann Georg Lang was born on 29 March 1691, in Beerfelden, Odenwaldkreis, Hesse, Germany, his father, Peter Lang, was 33 and his mother, M. Catharina Kumpf, was 29. He married Eva Steck on 16 January 1715, in Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Grand Duchy of Hesse, German Empire. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 10 November 1767, in Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Grand Duchy of Hesse, German Empire, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johann Georg Lang est né le 29 mars 1691, à Beerfelden, Odenwaldkreis, Hessen, Allemagne, son père, Peter Lang, avait 33 ans et sa mère, M. Catharina Kumpf, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Eva Steck le 16 janvier 1715, à Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 10 novembre 1767, à Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johann Georg Lang am 29. März 1691 in Beerfelden, Odenwaldkreis, Hessen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Peter Lang 33 und seine Mutter M. Catharina Kumpf 29. Er hat am 16. Januar 1715 in Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich Eva Steck geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 10. November 1767 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johann Georg Lang è nato il il 29 marzo 1691 nel Beerfelden, Odenwaldkreis, Hessen, Germania, il suo padre, Peter Lang, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, M. Catharina Kumpf, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Eva Steck il 16 gennaio 1715, nel Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 10 novembre 1767, nel Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Johann Georg Langが1691年3月29日にドイツヘセンオデンワルドクレイスビールフエルデンで生まれた時，父Peter Langは33歳，母M. Catharina Kumpfは29歳であった。1715年1月16日にデウトシェス レイチグロssハーゾグトゥム ヘセンクレイス エルバチビールフエルデンでEva Steckと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1767年11月10日にデウトシェス レイチグロssハーゾグトゥム ヘセンクレイス エルバチビールフエルデンで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Johann Georg Lang(이)가 1691년 3월 29일 독일 헤센 오데월드크레이스 브이르펠든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Peter Lang(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 M. Catharina Kumpf(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1715년 1월 16일 데우츠체스 라이치 그로ss헤어즈그투 헤센 크레이스 르바치 브이르펠든에서 Eva Steck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1767년 11월 10일 데우츠체스 라이치 그로ss헤어즈그투 헤센 크레이스 르바치 브이르펠든에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.29 March 1691–10 November 1767: 29 de março de 1691–10 de novembro de 1767

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Johann Georg Lang nasceu em 29 de março de 1691, em Beerfelden, Odenwaldkreis, Hessen, Alemanha, seu pai, Peter Lang, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, M. Catharina Kumpf, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Eva Steck em 16 de janeiro de 1715, em Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Império Alemão. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de novembro de 1767, em Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Império Alemão, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johann Georg Lang родился 29 марта 1691 г. в местности бирфелден, оденвалдкрис, Гессен, Германия, его отцу по имени Peter Lang было 33 года, а матери, которую звали M. Catharina Kumpf, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Eva Steck 16 января 1715 г. в местности бирфелден, крис эрбач, гроssхирзогтум хессен, деуцчесь раич. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 10 ноября 1767 г. в местности бирфелден, крис эрбач, гроssхирзогтум хессен, деуцчесь раич в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Johann Georg Lang nació el 29 de marzo de 1691, en Beerfelden, Odenwaldkreis, Hessen, Alemania, su papá, Peter Lang, tenía 33 años y su mamá, M. Catharina Kumpf, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Eva Steck el 16 de enero de 1715, en Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 10 de noviembre de 1767, en Beerfelden, Kreis Erbach, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutsches Reich, a la edad de 76 años.
